Ran a sample unit test with 10,000 records and the shuffle operation took <2ms.
Also as per the documentation the shuffle operation is O(N) complexity which is ok for our use case.
datasync watchdog process depends on module sync so we are ok here.
No. We dont need that. updated in next patch
yes we can. But gson is performance wise better than Jackson.

We replaced gson object mapper to jackson object mapper project wide, see:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-631+Spike%3A+Which+ObjectMapper+to+use+and+how+best+to+use+it+for+performance
https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-751

less then 2ms that is a lot faster then I expected! So agreed, based on the likely low numbers and fast processing shuffle is perfectly good to use!

I think we are better of not checking this.
let the system throw an exception (using .next() or .get()) if it gets this far without module source!

This will reduce complete of the code (a quality measure) and also prevent you from having to write testcases for which are really impossible scenarios...
NB: i'd rather call it sampleDataNodes or similar...

Note: I tend to use 'static' instead because it then fits one one line, its just a small personal preference (although it does go against the groovy over java guideline) But you can make up your own mind what you prefer
